label: Calibration, uncertainty & risk objectives

node.label: Calibration, uncertainty & risk objectives
node.definition: Training or post-processing aligns confidence with empirical correctness and asymmetric decision risk.
node.why_it_matters: The role of Calibration, uncertainty & risk objectives is to connect Distributions, uncertainty & beliefs, Statistics & experimental design to downstream capabilities including Knowledge distillation, pruning & sparsification, Calibration, uncertainty & risk evaluation, Adaptive depth, early exit & token skipping.
examples: substrate for Knowledge distillation, pruning & sparsification
substrate for Calibration, uncertainty & risk evaluation
substrate for Adaptive depth, early exit & token skipping
limitations: A tractable objective is a surrogate, so optimizing it can improve the score while leaving the intended behavior unchanged or worse. Its direct dependencies include Distributions, uncertainty & beliefs, Statistics & experimental design.
